codeReview.summary: Commit 582565e only fixes ONE of FOUR HTTP clients. The blocking issue from prior review stated that EgressFilter was NOT wired into vault.rs, web_search.rs, opa_policy.rs, and hybrid.rs. The fix addresses only hybrid.rs (RemoteExecutor). The other three files remain unfixed.

issues.description: VaultClient creates reqwest::Client directly without EgressFilter middleware. The http_client field is a plain Client (line 158-159), not wrapped with egress filtering. This was listed as a blocking issue in the prior review but was NOT fixed by commit 582565e.

SearchClient creates reqwest::Client directly without EgressFilter middleware. The client field (line 107-111) is a plain Client, not wrapped with egress filtering. This was listed as a blocking issue in the prior review but was NOT fixed by commit 582565e.

OpaPolicyEngine creates reqwest::Client directly without EgressFilter middleware. The client field (line 188-191) is a plain Client, not wrapped with egress filtering. This was listed as a blocking issue in the prior review but was NOT fixed by commit 582565e.

RemoteExecutor now has EgressFilter properly wired in. The with_egress_filter() constructor and is_egress_allowed() check at execute() entry were added correctly. However, this was only ONE of FOUR HTTP clients that needed fixing.

summary: FAIL - The fix in commit 582565e only addresses hybrid.rs (RemoteExecutor). The prior blocking issue explicitly identified FOUR HTTP clients needing EgressFilter wiring: vault.rs, web_search.rs, opa_policy.rs, and hybrid.rs. Only hybrid.rs was fixed. The other three files (vault.rs at line 40/158-159, web_search.rs at line 15/107-111, opa_policy.rs at line 21/188-191) still create reqwest::Client directly without EgressFilter middleware. The feature's description states 'The filter is applied as a middleware before every outbound HTTP request made by tools or agents' - this is not achieved with only hybrid.rs fixed.
